name: CI/CD Deployment Workflow
description: Export definitions, parameterize for environments, plan and apply with content-hash convergence. Includes variable library value set activation and notebook auto-binding validation. Also supports deploying from a cloned git repository.
prerequisites: Source workspace with items OR a git repo with .platform item definitions
Variable library with value sets named to match --env values (recommended)

steps.description: Export item definitions to local directory.
steps.notes: Alternative: clone a git repo containing .platform items (e.g., git clone https://github.com/org/fabric-items).

steps.description: Scan for GUIDs and generate parameter template.
steps.notes: Edit to fill environment-specific values. Use $ENV:VAR_NAME for CI/CD secrets.

steps.description: Pre-flight validation (checks for unknown types, duplicates, missing notebook-settings.json, parameter env coverage).
steps.notes: Runs offline — no API calls. Fix any errors/warnings before proceeding.

steps.description: Execute the deployment. Post-hooks auto-run: variable library value set activation (matching --env name), semantic model refresh, environment publish, lakehouse SQL endpoint poll.
steps.notes: Variable libraries deployed first; value set matching 'prod' auto-activated. Notebooks rebind via logicalId if notebook-settings.json is present.

steps.description: Verify the correct value set is active.
steps.notes: Optional verification step. The --env auto-activation should have already set it.

tips: Deploy from a git repo: git clone <URL> && fabio deploy apply --source <cloned-dir>/path/to/items --workspace $WS --env prod
The --source directory must contain subdirectories with .platform files (Fabric Git Integration format).
Variable library value sets are auto-activated when --env is specified. Name value sets to match env names (dev, test, prod).
Notebooks require notebook-settings.json for auto-binding. Without it, lakehouse dependencies won't resolve after deployment. fabio deploy validate warns about this.
If pipelines fail with 'invalid reference' errors, run: fabio deploy init-params --source <DIR> --resolve-connections --out params.json to auto-map connection GUIDs.
Use --dry-run on apply to preview without changes.
Use --delete-orphans to remove workspace items not in source (protected types require --allow-delete-types).
Use --query 'data.changes[?action==`Create`].name' to list items being created.
Use $ENV:VAR_NAME in parameters.json replace_value fields to inject secrets from CI/CD environment variables.
Use $workspace.id and $items.Type.Name.id for cross-item references that resolve at deploy time.
Check data.destructive field in plan output — true means deletions or force-all overwrites are planned.
For multi-workspace solutions: deploy each workspace separately with different --source and --workspace. Deploy storage/data workspaces first, then presentation workspaces.
Use --no-post-hooks to skip value set activation, semantic model refresh, and environment publish (useful for partial/incremental deploys).
Use --strategy bulk for large initial deployments (100+ items) to empty workspaces without Git integration. ~10x faster than per-item. Falls back to per-item for renames/deletes.
Use --strategy sequential (concurrency=1) when debugging API failures or rate-limit issues.
